title: Jaws of the Leviathan
summary: The Maw drops out of hyperspace over Malpaz — the world the heroes liberated, now targeted for Tarkin's demonstration of the gravity well superweapon they helped Varth unlock. Its crescent arms open and the sky begins to distort as asteroids shift orbit. With Oga Garra's pirate fleet and whatever allies remain, the heroes race to board the Leviathan and disable the gravity well array before it buries the planet under a rain of kinetic impactors. The campaign ends where the betrayal began. If Denia was abandoned in Adventure 2, the Draco showdown reveals her alive in his custody — broken but surviving. The heroes discover that Varth told them to leave her so he could hand her to the Inquisition.

gearNotes.reason: The Leviathan is an Imperial gravity well siege platform. Military-grade locks, blast doors, and security systems protecting the projector arrays at every turn.
The Final Hand likely requires accessing Leviathan's core systems — disabling the gravity well projector array, exposing the project, or extracting proof of the conspiracy.
Terminal bypasses under extreme time pressure. Every spike the party stocked in Adv9 gets burned here.
Coordinating inside an Imperial superweapon during Varth's betrayal. If comms are compromised, Varth uses that against the party.
The Approach involves getting into the facility — damaged ships need repair. The escape after the Final Hand requires a working ship.
The campaign climax. Multiple boss fights, Varth's betrayal, and the final escape. No resupply. Burn everything you have.
Sabotaging the Leviathan's gravity well generators — disabling projector systems, rigging reactor overloads, creating the opening to cripple the weapon or expose the project.
Cutting through Leviathan bulkheads, sealed sections, and emergency blast doors. Overload Mode for critical breaches.
Imperial superweapon = maximum droid and automated defense density. Every ion grenade stocked pays off here.
The final assault. Area denial against Imperial defenders in the facility corridors.
Covering movement through the facility and during the escape. Last chance to use them.
Multi-fight climax with no rest. Keep the party on their feet through the Betrayal and Final Hand.
Emergency escape routes, vertical shafts, or crossing damaged sections of the facility.
Power failures inside the Leviathan during sabotage. Emergency lighting may not last.

scenes.title: The Malpaz Corridor
scenes.subtitle: Into the Jaws
scenes.gmNotes: SOREN VEX DEATH: During the fleet battle approaching Malpaz, an Imperial missile locks onto the Banshee. Soren Vex throws the Requital into the missile's path, intercepting it. The explosion disables his ship — hull breached, engines dead, life support failing. He opens a comms channel to the Banshee. His last transmission: 'Maya. ... I know. ... Tell them I was — ' Static. A turbolaser from the Star Destroyer finishes the Requital before he can complete the sentence. Maya watches it happen. Do NOT let this moment be heroic in the conventional sense. Soren is not a hero. He's a coward who finally stopped running. The ambiguity of his unfinished sentence IS the emotional payload — Maya will never know what he was going to say. Play it quiet. Play it fast. The battle doesn't stop for grief.

replace.gmNotes: SOREN VEX DEATH (MAYA DEAD): During the fleet battle approaching Malpaz, an Imperial missile locks onto the Banshee. Soren Vex throws the Requital into the missile's path, intercepting it — he's protecting the Banshee and the crew Maya died for. The explosion disables his ship — hull breached, engines dead, life support failing. He opens a comms channel to the Banshee. His last transmission: 'I know she's gone. ... Tell them I was — ' Static. A turbolaser from the Star Destroyer finishes the Requital before he can complete the sentence. The crew carries a story with no living protagonist. The debt is paid to a ghost. Do NOT let this moment be heroic in the conventional sense. Soren is not a hero. He's a coward who finally stopped running. Play it quiet. Play it fast. The battle doesn't stop for grief.

npcs.name: Soren Vex
npcs.notes: Dies intercepting a missile meant for the Banshee. His ship the Requital is disabled by the explosion, then destroyed by a Star Destroyer turbolaser. Last words are unfinished. See campaign bible for full background.

parts.title: The Betrayal

scenes.title: Confronting Varth
scenes.subtitle: The Architect of Ruin
scenes.gmNotes: THE FINAL CONFRONTATION: This is not a villain monologue. Varth does not cackle, gloat, or explain his master plan — the heroes already know the plan. When they reach the Leviathan's command deck, Varth is working. He looks up, sees them, and something flickers across his face — not surprise, not fear. Recognition. 'You're here. Good. I wondered if you would be.' Play this quiet. He's tired. He's been running a military operation for months. He's not performing anymore. If a hero asks 'Why?' — and they will — his answer is honest and insufficient: 'Because I could. And because nobody else was going to do it right.' Varth will fight if cornered (use Command Varth stats — Controller/Leader, Tier 3). He will try to escape if possible. He will not beg. If the heroes offer surrender, he genuinely considers it — make the players believe he might take it. Whether he does depends on their relationship with him across the campaign. THE NICKNAME PAYOFF: If the GM has been tracking Varth's nickname habit ('Kid,' 'Pilot,' 'Heavy'), this is the moment he uses a hero's real name for the first time. Deploy it once, deliberately, in a moment of genuine emotion — not cruelty, not manipulation. Just a man acknowledging who he's talking to for the first time. It should land like a slap.

npcs.name: Admiral Gilder Varth
npcs.notes: Command Varth. No longer performing. Professional, efficient, tired. He doesn't hate the heroes — he's past them. The most dangerous Varth because he's not pretending to be anything.

threatBuild.name: Admiral Gilder Varth

npcEffects.masterful: Chassis damage + [Rattled] + target must move 1 zone (Varth's choice)
npcEffects.legendary: Chassis damage + [Rattled] + [Exposed] + target must move 1 zone

maneuver.name: Tactical Redeployment
maneuver.description: Move up to 3 allies within 2 zones each 1 zone (Varth's choice). Those allies gain [Optimized] on their next action.

gambits.name: Exploit Weakness
gambits.description: Target gains [Exposed] Tactical. Varth identifies and broadcasts the target's vulnerability to all Imperial forces in the zone.

gambits.name: Calculated Sacrifice
gambits.description: When an ally in Varth's zone would take damage, Varth may redirect the damage to a different ally in the same zone. The sacrificed ally takes full damage. Varth takes none.

exploit.name: Contingency Order
exploit.trigger: An ally within 2 zones is targeted by an attack.
exploit.description: That ally may immediately move 1 zone before the attack resolves.

threatBuild.notes: Varth commands from behind cover and uses Imperial troops as shields. His 'Calculated Sacrifice' gambit is mechanically reflective of his character — he will spend allies to survive. Resist 7 means he is nearly impossible to read or manipulate socially. The heroes must beat him tactically, not rhetorically. If reduced below half Vitality, Varth attempts to reach an escape pod. If cornered with no escape, he surrenders — but only if a hero uses his trick: calls him by his first name. 'Gilder. It's over.' That breaks through.

parts.description: Final confrontation with Varth aboard the Leviathan. The Draco showdown resolves the Inquisitor's arc.

append.description: DENIA ABANDONED PATH: The heroes discover Denia alive in Draco's custody — interrogated for years, broken but surviving. This is the campaign's deepest gut-punch: Varth told them to leave her, and they listened. He handed her to the Empire the moment they looked away.
DENIA RESCUED PATH: Denia was captured by Draco in Adv7 and potentially rescued in Adv9 — the Draco showdown plays as a nemesis encounter without the betrayal-reveal layer.
